reviews.author: PARTHA BHATTACHARJEE
reviews.text: One of the main point of interest of Halong Bay cruise is exploration of this natural cave named Sung Sot Cave. After a short ride from the cruise vessel on the Tender boats one lands at the entry point. Then a steep staircase climb of about 100 steps more or less, to the main entrance of the cave. In between there are formalities of tickets purchasing etc but those were well taken care of by our guide. The staircase is quite steep and we were advised against undertaking this activity for those we are weak and not confident enough. However, around 10 of us confidently took this journey and completed successfully. No women in the team. There are three caves in the total journey, one small, one medium and the large one is huge with floor to ceiling distance of more than 50/60 feet, if not more. The natural formations inside are exciting and unique to watch. Some natural formation mimics human and animal shapes if viewed from a particular angle and he guide explains this with pleasure. During the last stage also there is a steep climb of approximately 50/100 steps which is slightly demanding after the long passage. Although, there are no significant gradient negotiation within the cave, there is considerable walking on uneven rocky pathways and uneven steps and narrow passages. Lastly a word of caution : The starting point and finishing points are different and once the climb is started it needs to be finished - no returning midway - as the tender boat goes to the exit point after deboarding all tourists at the entry point. Highly recommended for capable and physically fit persons.

reviews.author: Maria Georgieva
reviews.text: It was a way too touristic than I expected, we spent the day waiting in cues and in hurry just to wait for the next spot. Nature is beautiful though, it is impressive the way it has touched this place, but it is turned into an attraction for massive tourism, which deprives the place from it's beauty and serenity. The queue literally starts from the entrance and it doesn't finish till the end. My partner was here couple of years ago when there were not so many touristic groups and people visiting and he says the experience was quite different and peaceful.

reviews.author: Chetan
reviews.text: Sung Sot Cave is truly amazing and definitely lives up to its name! It is the biggest cave I have ever seen. When you first enter, it looks small, but then it opens up into massive rooms that look like a giant natural cathedral. The colorful lights inside make the rock formations look very cool. You should book a tour guide because it's fun and you need some context to explore the caves properly. We had a really nice guide who explained things and made the trip fun. He showed us many imaginary characters in the rocks, like Santa Claus, a snake, and a hand, which was great. Overall, the caves are well-maintained and very fun to explore. I highly recommend visiting

reviews.author: MAX MAX
reviews.text: The caves have already to them and some of the natural carvings done by the natural weathering. Is so unique and wonderful, that each person was seeing things differently. Isn't that a mystery of life...or just a natural occurrence...in our party we were all seeing things differently. It really is a unique experience. Well worth the a visit. Its a long day, and we were provided lunch and a snack later on the day. We are in a largish group, really nice group so good tome with all.

reviews.author: Earth Wanderer
reviews.text: Sung Sot Cave: Truly a Cave of "Surprise" and Grandeur Sung Sot Cave, or Surprise Cave, is absolutely a must-see highlight of any trip to Halong Bay, and it certainly lives up to its name. The journey begins with a short but steep climb (about 50 steps!) to the entrance, which offers rewarding panoramic views of the bay's magnificent karst landscape—a great photo opportunity right off the bat. The cave entrance itself is small and unassuming, which makes the reveal of the interior even more dramatic. Once inside, the scale of the two main chambers is truly awe-inspiring. The cavern is vast, with ceilings soaring up to 30 meters high. It feels like walking into a massive, natural underground opera house. The stalactites and stalagmites are incredibly impressive, having been shaped over millions of years into formations that resemble mythical creatures, elephants, and a warrior's horse and sword (linked to a local legend). The strategically placed colorful lighting highlights the intricate details and adds a magical, otherworldly atmosphere. My only reason for a 4-star instead of a 5-star rating is its extreme popularity. The cave can get very crowded, particularly in the middle of the day, which sometimes diminishes the peaceful, awe-inspiring feeling. The sheer volume of visitors means you have to move through the chambers at a steady pace, limiting the time you have to fully absorb the quiet grandeur of the formations. However, the sheer size and artistic beauty of this natural wonder make it an essential stop. Go early if you can to beat the crowds!

reviews.author: elby
reviews.text: This was our second stop of the tour and its a network of 2 small caves going into the final larger cave. Views are pretty picturesque but the long queue just to enter may deter you from entering. Took us about a good half hour before we could get into the entrance but it was definitely worth the wait. The hike up is gradual so its a pretty relaxing walk that many will be able to complete, you enter on one side of the island and exit at another point so the guides did mention that you need to complete the whole stretch to get back into the ship. Temperature inside the cave was nice and cooling which was very pleasant.

reviews.author: Aida Lifestyle Traveler
reviews.text: Visiting Sung Sot Cave was truly one of the highlights of our time in Ha Long Bay. And the name says it all — “Sung Sot” means Surprising, and it absolutely lives up to it. ✨ From the moment you step inside, you’re surrounded by massive limestone formations that feel almost unreal. The cave is enormous, dramatic, and beautifully illuminated, making every corner look like a natural masterpiece. 🕯️⛰️ Walking through the chambers felt like entering another world — towering stalactites, unique rock shapes, and breathtaking open spaces that leave you in complete awe. At one point, you just stop talking… because nature speaks louder than words. 🤍 The view from outside the cave is just as magical, overlooking the emerald waters and limestone islands of Ha Long Bay. 🌊 Sung Sot Cave isn’t just a sightseeing stop — it’s an experience that reminds you how powerful and artistic nature truly is. Definitely a must-visit if you’re exploring Ha Long Bay. ✨

reviews.author: Aprajita Singh
reviews.text: Before visiting, we were repeatedly told that exploring this cave would be extremely tiring and uncomfortable. Many people described it as suffocating and physically demanding, which set our expectations quite low. However, the actual experience turned out to be surprisingly pleasant and very different from what we had anticipated. The walk through the cave was not as exhausting as expected, and at no point did it feel claustrophobic or difficult to breathe. The cave was well-ventilated, and the space felt more open than we had imagined. One of the most striking aspects was the presence of natural sunlight filtering in through certain openings. These sunlit spots not only made the cave feel less enclosed but also added a beautiful visual element, making the experience feel calm and enjoyable rather than intimidating. Overall, the cave visit was a positive and memorable experience. It challenged common assumptions and proved that sometimes places described as difficult or uncomfortable can turn out to be quite manageable and even enjoyable. The balance of natural light, airflow, and ease of movement made this visit far more pleasant than expected, and it was definitely worth exploring.

reviews.author: Giorgio Settele
reviews.text: A magical place where nature has created an architectural masterpiece. Absolutely to visit. Wear comfortable shoes and be prepared for a nice climb with several steps. Enjoy nature!

reviews.author: Peter Lovic
reviews.text: It was nice that the cave is accessible by boat, on the island, it is large and a nicely designed tour, a must see, for us it is less rare, since we have many caves and larger ones in Slovakia, I have also been around people who discovered caves since childhood, in any case it was a nice experience here

reviews.author: Stevent Febriandy
reviews.text: Visiting Sung Sot Cave in Ha Long Bay was an unforgettable experience. The cave showcases the beauty of karst formations, with impressive stalactites and stalagmites formed over millions of years. Walking through its vast chambers feels surreal, as nature’s artistry unfolds at every turn. What amazed us most was the panoramic view at the exit, overlooking the iconic limestone islands rising from emerald waters. It truly gives a new perspective on how powerful and patient nature is in shaping such wonders. A must-visit spot in Ha Long Bay that leaves you in awe and appreciation.

reviews.author: Anilgowda
reviews.text: Sung Sot (Surprise) Cave in Ha Long Bay is a must-visit attraction that truly lives up to its name. The cave features massive chambers with dramatic stalactite and stalagmite formations, beautifully lit to highlight natural details. The walk inside is smooth and well-guided, with multiple viewing points offering stunning scenery. As you climb up to the entrance and exit, the panoramic views of Ha Long Bay are spectacular. Best visited in the morning to avoid crowds. Overall, a breathtaking experience showcasing nature’s beauty and one of the highlights of any Ha Long Bay cruise.

reviews.author: Nick Qi
reviews.text: Not a large cave system but a few chambers with pedestrian paths snaking around them. However, the location is impressive: located on a remote limestone island, the cave entrance is high above the waterline, and the exit is even higher. The latter offers a bird's eye view of the bay below where a seemingly endless stream of boats delivering visitors to the shore for the cave visit. The limestone inside the cave is well illuminated by both artificial lights and sunlight through the exit, but there is no vegetation found inside meaning the sunlight is not consistent enough. Concrete stairs and pathways allowed visitors of all ages to access the entire cave. The difficulty level is very low. Only the stairs leading to the entrance and coming down from the exit are a bit steep but traffic is generally slow due to overcrowding. Some strategic photo op points are highlighted. Separate docks are built for disembark and boarding to allow an one-way foot traffic through the tourist attraction. Facilities are available at the both ends but mostly the exit end for refreshments and souvenirs.

reviews.author: Utkersh Bora ubmuse
reviews.text: Walking into Sung Sốt Cave (which literally means “Surprise Cave”) feels like nature set you up with low expectations… and then dramatically pulled back the curtain yelling, “TA-DAAA!” From the outside, it’s a modest climb and a calm entrance. Inside? Boom. Massive chambers, towering stalactites, dramatic lighting, and ceilings so high you instinctively look up and whisper “okay wow.” The scale of the place keeps escalating — just when you think you’ve seen the main part, the cave says, “Hold my limestone.” The formations look like they’ve been sculpted by an artist with too much time and excellent taste. Every guide has a story for each rock shape, and even if you don’t remember them later, you’ll remember the feeling of being very small in a very impressive space. Yes, it’s popular. Yes, there are people. But the cave is big enough to absorb the crowd without losing its magic. And once you step out and see Ha Long Bay again? That transition hits hard.

reviews.author: Tanzim Khan
reviews.text: Sung Sot Cave, also known as Surprise Cave, is the largest and one of the most breathtaking caves in Ha Long Bay. The scale of its rock formations is incredible, with stunning stalactites, stalagmites, and vast chambers that will definitely surprise you. It’s a dry cave, making it easy to explore on foot, though the path includes a few moderate climbs. As you exit, you’ll enter the Royal Garden, a beautiful area with a serene lake and lush greenery. A visit here is a must, especially since this site is part of the UNESCO World Heritage site. (watch my videos to see the beauty of this place)

reviews.author: Tia
reviews.text: We had a really adventurous experience visiting Sung Sot Cave. It’s a popular attraction for a reason. The caves are impressive, with beautiful formations and a cool temperature inside, which was a nice break from the heat. There isn’t much climbing involved, just some stairs along the way, so it’s pretty manageable for most people. There are also a few small, squishy shortcuts through narrow paths if you’re feeling brave, which adds a bit of fun to the visit. We really loved the experience and would definitely recommend it.

reviews.author: Ashesh Bharadwaj
reviews.text: This was part of the Halong Bay day cruise. I haven't seen such large cave in my life. They are huge. Properly lit and marked so that despite the crowd movement is easy. You can get a picture for free on the key chain and buy the photo frame for small price. I think they are worth it.

reviews.author: Prabhakar Aradalli
reviews.text: I visited here during Feb 1st week 2026.our guide from hera luxury cruise with 25 members on board lead the team and explained about the caves and its significance..we all spent a quality time and explored and got some knowledge as well

reviews.author: Avinesh Jayakumar
reviews.text: A little more underwhelming than expected as the wait was awhile to get up the stairs. The cave itself was pretty and interesting to be in and see thousands of years of rock formations. Was a part of a Ha Long Bay cruise, but i think was a decent location to start the day. But at least once u get to some viewpoints u get to see the bay and the boats moving about.

reviews.author: Raymond Lai
reviews.text: A really worthy hike though incredible natural caves. You need a tour or a boat to access the island. I recommend visiting with a guide so you can get additional context on the history of the cave system. They will offer to take a photo of you to put inside a photo keychain. Keychain is free but they will sell you full size prints. Your can decline the print and just take the keychain with no strings attached. It's pretty low pressure.

reviews.author: Sai Patnaik
reviews.text: Here is a more personalised, yet still professional, version: Sung Sot Cave – Personal Snapshot My visit to Sung Sot Cave was genuinely impressive. The moment I entered the first chamber, the scale and natural architecture immediately delivered that “wow” factor. As I moved deeper inside, the illuminated rock formations, the cool atmosphere and the well-designed walkways made the entire experience seamless and highly engaging. The final viewpoint overlooking Halong Bay was the perfect wrap-up. Overall, it felt like one of those standout moments that truly elevates a Halong Bay trip.

reviews.author: Suchitra S. Karnani
reviews.text: The Sung Sot caves are mesmerizing limestone caves formed by magnificent Stalactites and Stalagmites. It's located on the Bo Hon island and a visit to these caves is a part of the 2-day itnerary when you go on a Halong Bay cruise It is not to be skipped ...the stairs may seem too much initially but tye lines move slowly and when you get inside ...you'll be spell bound ......there are not enough words to describe this natural beauty !

reviews.author: Jayashree Reddy
reviews.text: Breathtaking scenic views and sunset backdrop . We were mesmerized by the beauty of the natural landscape and the sandstone and lime deposits structural caves over the years giving it a defined look . Vacation was truly amazing with the star ferry and Halong Bay attractions .

reviews.author: Ravi Teja M
reviews.text: It is a very well maintained cave the entry and exit are well organised to avoid jams. There is also a spot where they captured your photo and give you a free keychain. They also print a photo and engrave in a frame which is worth 100k dong and you can buy if you are interested which can be collected near the exit

reviews.author: Pratik Nagarikar
reviews.text: Its suprisingly big cave in sea. You need to climb around 500 stairs overall. But the cave is bigger than expectation. Its one time visit in cave

reviews.author: Joanna Zukowska
reviews.text: The cave itself is stunning and huge. One of the biggest i've seen. Pretty incredible what mother nature can do. However, the place is overcrowded. We were there in low season and queues were reallly big. Tons of ships arriving one after another.

reviews.author: Gaurav Agrawal
reviews.text: A big limestone cave located in Ha long bay in north eastern Vietnam This cave is known as surprise cave in english Visit to this cave is included in package to Ha long bay visit This cave is very big and beautiful You need to ascend many steps to reach here The cave is well lit inside that makes convenient to visit You need to know basic geology to understand the structures and formation of this cave You can get clicked by professionals and get instant key chain photo inside the cave The ticket cost is also included in ticket packages Such type of caves are very common in Vietnam but rare in India,so you must visit this cabe and don’t chose to remain in cruise while your group went to visit

reviews.author: Mohamed Aslam
reviews.text: We visited these caves as the last stop of our tour, and they were absolutely breathtaking. The rock formations were stunning, especially the final cave, which, according to our guide, is over 10,000 square meters in size. It was also noticeably cooler inside compared to the outside heat, making the visit even more enjoyable. Definitely worth it!

reviews.author: JAVIER PD
reviews.text: Visiting Sung Sot Cave was one of the highlights of my trip to Ha Long Bay. The moment you enter, you understand why it’s called the “Surprise Cave”—each chamber gets larger and more impressive than the last. The rock formations are beautifully illuminated, making it easy to appreciate the stunning stalactites and stalagmites shaped by thousands of years of natural processes. The cave is spacious and well-maintained, with a clear walking path that guides you through the different sections. I loved how each chamber felt like its own world, especially the main hall, which opens up into a massive natural cathedral. The views from the exit, overlooking the bay, are also incredible. It can get a bit crowded depending on the time of day, but the beauty of the cave makes it absolutely worth it. If you’re exploring Ha Long Bay, Sung Sot Cave is a must-see attraction—spectacular, unique, and unforgettable.

reviews.author: Jin
reviews.text: The last admission is around 4 PM. (sunset 17:15 on november) The cave has three main viewing sections, with the Royal Chamber being the largest and most impressive. From the point where you get off the boat, you enter by walking slowly through the cave, and it takes about 45–60 minutes to exit and return to the pier.
